user_prompt: Use argparse, allow the user to specify any `*.json`file as the input, and an output path. If the output path is not specified, then the output path is the same place as the `*.json` file but with an extension like `*.sum.md`. If the input file name ended `*.trans.json` then remove the `trans` part of it.
The user can specify a `*.wav` file instead, in which case, import `thefly_summarize` and use it to do the first step that generates a transcription first. The intermediate transcribed file is kept.
Assume `OPENAI_API_KEY` is already set in the environment
Use OpenAI’s API platform to perform the summarization
agent_reply: - `.wav` input first calls [scripts/thefly_transcription.py](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_transcription.py), keeps `meeting.trans.json`, then writes `meeting.sum.md`.
- Uses `OPENAI_API_KEY` and OpenAI’s `/v1/responses` API for summarization.
I also added optional `--chunking-strategy` support to `thefly_transcription.py`, and the summarizer uses `chunking_strategy="auto"` for WAV transcription.
I didn’t run a real API call. `src/version.c` is still dirty from existing build/version tooling.

user_prompt: have a look at `def append_wav_info_icmt(wav_path: Path, comment: Optional[str]) -> None:` inside `scripts\thefly_audio_decoder.py`
for `scripts\thefly_transcription.py`, when saving the JSON file, add a few final json key-value pairs as "meta_type" and "meta_comment"
first, determine if this is a meeting or call or memo from the file name. If this is successful, we can set "meta_type" to "meeting" or "memo". Otherwise, use "unknown"
extract the comment out of the `*.wav` file and save it into `meta_comment`, this could be caller information, or it could be a memo-type. Otherwise leave the string blank.

for `scripts\thefly_summarize.py`, use the "meta_type" to determine a prompt for the AI. For the meeting type, make the prompt more generic, less specific to engineering. The `meta_comment` field might be useful in determining if the memo was a "reminder" or an "idea" or etc, see `inc\defs.h` `MEMO_TYPE_*`
agent_reply: - Adds `meta_type` and `meta_comment` to saved transcription JSON.
- Reads `meta_comment` from the WAV `INFO/ICMT` chunk written by `append_wav_info_icmt`.
- The WAV comment reader streams chunks instead of loading the whole WAV.
- WAV transcription path now preserves those metadata fields in the intermediate `.trans.json`.
- Memo prompts use `meta_comment` as a subtype hint for `note`, `todo`, `journal`, `idea`, or `reminder`.
Also tested filename classification and WAV `ICMT` metadata extraction locally. No live OpenAI API call was run. `src/version.c` is still dirty from existing build/version tooling.

commit_message: python scripts alignment, clean up and comments

user_prompt: a base class to represent AI models
and classes that act as that base class
the base class presents to the caller a way to transcribe any audio file specified by file path, and writes a JSON file in the specified path
the base class presents to the caller a way to summarize any transcription json file, and writes a markdown file in the specified path
models don't have to implement both things, just throw a not implemented exception
the scripts `scripts\thefly_summarize.py` and `scripts\thefly_transcription.py` will be modified to use this
there will be a factory function that takes model names and returns the correct actual model class

each model class is responsible for all the requests, which is all done through OpenAI's API, BUT, most of the models are actually using Ollama with the server pretending to be the OpenAI API but using the local IP
For actual OpenAI models, assume the API key is in the environment already
For transcription, have support for `gpt-4o-transcribe` and `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize` `Whisper` and `Faster-Whisper` and `Parakeet`
For summarization, have support for `gpt-4o-mini`, Qwen 3, Gemma 3, Llama 3.3, DeepSeek-R1
agent_reply: - `AIModel` base class for transcription to JSON and summarization to Markdown.
- Local OpenAI-compatible/Ollama-style models: Whisper, Faster-Whisper, Parakeet, Qwen 3, Gemma 3, Llama 3.3, DeepSeek-R1.
- `create_model(...)` factory, including tagged local ids like `qwen3:8b` and `llama3.3:70b`.

Refactored [scripts/thefly_transcription.py](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_transcription.py>) and [scripts/thefly_summarize.py](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_summarize.py>) to use the model layer. I also kept the old script-level functions/constants as thin compatibility wrappers so [scripts/thefly_desktop.py](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_desktop.py>) still imports cleanly.
python -m py_compile scripts\models.py scripts\thefly_transcription.py scripts\thefly_summarize.py scripts\thefly_desktop.py
No live API requests were made. There are still unrelated pre-existing dirty files in the worktree; I left those alone.
- `AIModel` base class for transcription to JSON and summarization to Markdown.
- Local OpenAI-compatible/Ollama-style models: Whisper, Faster-Whisper, Parakeet, Qwen 3, Gemma 3, Llama 3.3, DeepSeek-R1.
- `create_model(...)` factory, including tagged local ids like `qwen3:8b` and `llama3.3:70b`.

Refactored [scripts/thefly_transcription.py](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_transcription.py>) and [scripts/thefly_summarize.py](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_summarize.py>) to use the model layer. I also kept the old script-level functions/constants as thin compatibility wrappers so [scripts/thefly_desktop.py](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_desktop.py>) still imports cleanly.
python -m py_compile scripts\models.py scripts\thefly_transcription.py scripts\thefly_summarize.py scripts\thefly_desktop.py
No live API requests were made. There are still unrelated pre-existing dirty files in the worktree; I left those alone.

user_prompt: how do I specify the size of the model?
agent_reply: Use the model’s Ollama tag in `--model`. The factory preserves tagged local model names.
If the input to `thefly_summarize.py` is audio, specify transcription and summary sizes separately:
The part after `:` is passed through as the actual OpenAI-compatible/Ollama model id.

user_prompt: `scripts\thefly_transcription.py` got a `Prompt is not supported for diarization models`, so please do not include a prompt if the model is the diaization model
agent_reply: `OpenAICompatibleTranscriptionModel` now has `supports_prompt = True`, and `GPT4oTranscribeDiarizeModel` sets `supports_prompt = False`. That means `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize` skips both explicit `--prompt` and the automatic filename-derived prompt.
python -m py_compile scripts\models.py scripts\thefly_transcription.py scripts\thefly_summarize.py scripts\thefly_desktop.py

`OpenAICompatibleTranscriptionModel` now has `supports_prompt = True`, and `GPT4oTranscribeDiarizeModel` sets `supports_prompt = False`. That means `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize` skips both explicit `--prompt` and the automatic filename-derived prompt.
python -m py_compile scripts\models.py scripts\thefly_transcription.py scripts\thefly_summarize.py scripts\thefly_desktop.py

user_prompt: use argparse, take in a path to an audio file
also allow the user to specify an output file path, if the output file path is not specified, use the input file's directory.
also let the user choose a silence level, but this is optional, use a sensible default
open the file, decode it into a PCM stream, stereo if the file is stereo
analyze the file into a list of silence chunks, each chunk represented by a start and stop time, and can provide a duration
divid the file into big sections, if the file is under 22 minutes, leave it alone, otherwise, find the best way to split the file so that each section is between 12 minutes and 18 minutes.
now we know where the rough split points are, go look around the first split point, within a search window of 2 minutes, find the largest silence period.
The middle of the largest silence is where we split. take the audio before the split, go through it again ensuring all silence periods are 3 seconds or shorter.
save it as "<output file path without extension>.part1.<wav>"
then repeat, saving each processed large section as "<output file path without extension>.part<n>.<wav>"
if a section is shorter than 22 minutes, don't bother dividing it, but still remove silence from it.
This function will either return the original file path if the original file is under 22 minutes and thus need no splitting, or, it returns a list of new file paths.
`scripts\thefly_transcription.py` needs to import this new file and new function, and use it before submitting to the AI model, as the models are rejecting large files.
agent_reply: - Decodes audio to 16-bit PCM WAV, preserving channel count, using WAV directly when possible and `ffmpeg` otherwise.
- Detects silence chunks as `SilenceChunk(start, stop)` with `.duration`.
- Leaves audio alone if it is <= 22 minutes and returns the original path.
- For longer audio, splits into roughly 12-18 minute sections, choosing the midpoint of the largest silence within 2 minutes of each rough split.
- Caps long silence inside each part to 3 seconds.

Transcription now calls `shorten_audio_file()` before submitting audio. If the file is split, it transcribes each part and combines the results into one JSON with combined `text`, adjusted `segments`, and a `parts` record.
Also updated [thefly_summarize.py](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_summarize.py:107>) so summarizing an audio file uses the same transcription wrapper.
python -m py_compile scripts\audio_file_shortener.py scripts\thefly_transcription.py scripts\thefly_summarize.py scripts\thefly_desktop.py scripts\models.py
I also ran synthetic WAV tests: short audio returned the original path; a 23-minute test file split into two WAV parts and reduced the long silence around the split. No live AI/API calls were made.
- Decodes audio to 16-bit PCM WAV, preserving channel count, using WAV directly when possible and `ffmpeg` otherwise.
- Detects silence chunks as `SilenceChunk(start, stop)` with `.duration`.
- Leaves audio alone if it is <= 22 minutes and returns the original path.
- For longer audio, splits into roughly 12-18 minute sections, choosing the midpoint of the largest silence within 2 minutes of each rough split.
- Caps long silence inside each part to 3 seconds.

Transcription now calls `shorten_audio_file()` before submitting audio. If the file is split, it transcribes each part and combines the results into one JSON with combined `text`, adjusted `segments`, and a `parts` record.
Also updated [thefly_summarize.py](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_summarize.py:107>) so summarizing an audio file uses the same transcription wrapper.
python -m py_compile scripts\audio_file_shortener.py scripts\thefly_transcription.py scripts\thefly_summarize.py scripts\thefly_desktop.py scripts\models.py
I also ran synthetic WAV tests: short audio returned the original path; a 23-minute test file split into two WAV parts and reduced the long silence around the split. No live AI/API calls were made.

user_prompt: how many json files and how many md files are made? I'm hoping your implementation took several resulting json files and merged them into one, and then one big json file gets summarized into one md file
agent_reply: For a long audio file run through `scripts\thefly_summarize.py`:
- JSON files: **1** combined transcription JSON, e.g. `recording.trans.json`
It does **not** write separate per-part JSON files. Each part is transcribed in memory, then merged into one JSON object:
- `parts[]`, where each entry contains that part’s raw transcription result
Then `thefly_summarize.py` summarizes that single combined JSON into one Markdown file.

commit_message: python changed to use newer scripts

user_prompt: One wrinkle: thefly_desktop.py still passes the default API URLs from the wrapper scripts. For summarization that is currently https://api.openai.com/v1/responses, even though qwen3:14b itself is an Ollama-style model name. So the model default is local-ish, but the API URL default looks mismatched unless overridden elsewhere.
can you fix this? that real URL is supposed to be burried into `models.py` inside one of the cloud models classes, which is the whole point of having those classes
agent_reply: Fixed. The wrapper scripts no longer own OpenAI endpoint defaults.
- [scripts/models.py](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/models.py:18): added OpenAI endpoint defaults inside the model layer.
- [scripts/models.py](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/models.py:621): OpenAI transcription models now default to `https://api.openai.com/v1/audio/transcriptions`.
- [scripts/models.py](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/models.py:840): OpenAI Responses summary models now default to `https://api.openai.com/v1/responses`.
- [scripts/thefly_desktop.py](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_desktop.py:323): desktop flow now passes `api_url=None`, so model classes choose their own endpoint.
- Removed the wrapper-level `DEFAULT_API_URL` constants from transcription/summarization scripts.
Verified with compile checks and a no-network smoke test:
So `thefly_desktop.py` now defaults to local `qwen3:14b` via Ollama for summarization, while cloud models still resolve to OpenAI from their own classes.

user_prompt: Cleaner long-term fix would be adding --transcription-model and --summary-model flags to thefly_desktop.py.
Please do this, but leave the defaults blank in this file, have the other scripts detect the blank and fill it in with the defaults from `models.py`
agent_reply: Added `--transcription-model` and `--summary-model` to [scripts/thefly_desktop.py](C:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_desktop.py:715). Their defaults are blank strings in that file, and the model resolution now happens in:

I also changed the desktop transcription call to pass `response_format=None` and `chunking_strategy=None`, so each model class can choose its own defaults. That keeps local `faster-whisper` working while `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize` still gets diarized output and auto chunking from its class.
Verified with `py_compile`, `--help`, and a no-network smoke test confirming blank desktop defaults resolve to the `models.py` defaults.
